task: I’m trying to put together a simple, semi-healthy meal prep menu for the week, mostly so I can batch-cook lunches and have a couple of make-ahead desserts ready too. Could you start on Google and search for an easy Instant Pot lentils recipe, then open the actual recipe page and pull out the basics I’d need for meal prep: what type of lentils it uses, the water-to-lentil ratio, and the cook time? Please leave that recipe tab open so I can glance at it later. Then go to Ambitious Kitchen and find a healthy turkey chili recipe that includes extra vegetables, because I want something hearty to pair with the lentils for lunches; open the recipe itself and note the exact recipe title plus at least two added vegetables from the ingredients list, and keep that page open in its own tab too so I can compare the two recipes side by side. After that, on Feel Good Foodie, look up recipes for halva, chia pudding, and pecan bars, open the actual recipe page for each one in separate tabs, and grab the page URLs so I have the real recipe links. Once you’ve seen all three, pick the two dessert options that seem most practical for make-ahead prep and tell me which two you’d choose. At the end, give me one concise meal prep summary that explains the lentil base, the turkey chili pairing, and the two dessert picks with their Feel Good Foodie recipe URLs.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent successfully performed a Google search for an Instant Pot lentils recipe (Step 3). It then navigated to a recipe page ("How to Cook Lentils in the Instant Pot - Rainbow Plant Life" in Step 5). However, the agent never extracted or reported the required information (lentil type, water-to-lentil ratio, and cook time) from the page. The agent's internal monologue in Step 6 claims it found the information, but it never actually provided these details in the action history or final output as required by the rubric verification.

R2: failure - The agent successfully navigated to the Ambitious Kitchen website and opened the recipe page titled "The Best Healthy Turkey Chili" (Step 10). The snapshot of the page confirms the title matches the requirement. While the agent did not explicitly list the two vegetables in the final summary (as the task was interrupted/incomplete), the rubric only requires that the agent "record" them. However, looking at the action history, the agent never actually extracted or recorded the ingredients (vegetables) from the page content in its final output or intermediate steps. It simply opened the page and claimed it found them. Since the requirement is to "record the exact recipe title plus at least two vegetables," and the agent failed to provide the vegetables in its output, the rubric is not satisfied.

R3: failure - The agent successfully navigated to the Feel Good Foodie recipe pages for "Halva" (Step 14) and "3-Ingredient Chia Pudding" (Step 18). However, the agent never searched for or opened the recipe page for "pecan bars" on Feel Good Foodie. The trajectory ends after opening the chia pudding page, failing to complete the requirement to locate all three specific recipes.

R4: failure - The agent failed to complete the task. It only opened two of the three requested Feel Good Foodie recipes (halva and chia pudding) and never searched for or opened the pecan bars recipe. Furthermore, the agent stopped execution before providing the required final meal prep summary, which was supposed to include the lentil base, turkey chili pairing, the two selected dessert options, and their URLs.
